comment.body:
in a regime like this, a hot inflation means markets price tighter policy and/or higher term premium pushing 10y yields up, 
but higher discount rates plus margin pressure, making equities down
comment.raw_body:
in a regime like this, a hot inflation means markets price tighter policy and/or higher term premium pushing 10y yields up,

comment.body:
when the beta is positive, thats when demand shock dominates. thats the classic "risk off/risk on" environment. 
good growth news means earnings optimism, higher expected rates and less easing. this makes both equities and yields up.

if bad growth news come, thats the "flight to safety"  scenario, with yields down, equitites down
comment.raw_body:
